commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

repository.description: Manage multiple Anthropic Claude accounts with Claude Code CLI — easily switch accounts in seconds.

commit.message:
feat: action queue, tasks, health scores, stall detection, win/loss analysis

Major feature additions transforming the CRM from a tracker to an action-driving system:

1. Smart Action Queue ("What To Do Next")
   - Prioritized action feed at top of dashboard
   - Surfaces: stalled deals, expiring permits, unpaid commissions,
     overdue tasks, aging leads
   - Clickable items navigate to relevant sections

2. Tasks & Reminders
   - New tasks table (DB migration) with priority, due dates, project links
   - Full CRUD UI with filter tabs: Active, My Tasks, Overdue, Done
   - Realtime sync via Supabase postgres_changes
   - Overdue alert banner on dashboard

3. Deal Velocity & Stall Detection
   - Computes avg time per pipeline stage (lead, in_progress)
   - Flags projects at 2x stage average as "stalled"
   - Orange "Stalled" badge on Kanban cards
   - "Stalled Deals" metric + "Avg Lead Time" / "Avg Build Time" on dashboard

4. Customer Health Score
   - Scores active projects 0-100 based on: stage duration, permit status,
     revenue collection, interconnection delays
   - Green/yellow/red health dot on project table rows and Kanban cards
   - Health distribution metric on dashboard (green/yellow/red counts)

5. Win/Loss Analysis
   - close_reason + close_notes columns added to projects (DB migration)
   - CloseReasonModal pops up after completing or cancelling a project
   - Dropdown of common win/loss reasons + freeform notes
   - "Skip" option to not block workflow

6. Expanded Dashboard Metrics
   - Projects row 2: Win Rate, Stalled Deals, Avg Lead Time, Avg Build Time,
     Health Distribution
   - All metrics now total 23 KPIs across 4 categories

Files changed:
- app/components/ActionQueue.tsx — NEW, smart action feed
- app/components/TasksPanel.tsx — NEW, tasks CRUD with filters
- app/components/CloseReasonModal.tsx — NEW, win/loss analysis modal
- lib/healthScore.ts — NEW, health score computation + colors
- app/page.tsx — integrated all features, new state, derived metrics
- app/components/KanbanBoard.tsx — stall badges + health dots on cards
- lib/projectService.ts — close_reason/close_notes in updateProject type
- supabase/migrations/20260306_tasks_and_close_reason.sql — NEW

DB migrations (already run):
- ALTER TABLE projects ADD COLUMN close_reason TEXT, close_notes TEXT
- CREATE TABLE tasks (with RLS policy + indexes)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix SSL wrong version error in email service

Derive secure flag from port instead of EMAIL_SECURE env var.
Port 465 uses direct SSL (secure: true), port 587 uses STARTTLS (secure: false).
A mismatched secure/port combination caused the SSL handshake to fail.

commit.message:
Fix checkbox group (multi-select) handling in form filler

LinkedIn forms with "Select all that apply" questions use checkbox groups
inside fieldsets. The fieldset handler only clicked one label (radio behavior).
Now detects checkbox vs radio fieldsets and splits comma-separated AI answers
to click multiple labels. Also added "consent" to auto-check keyword list.

commit.message:
fix(cardapio): block non-preferred proteins + caloric safety net

Problem 1 — Frango in complemento proteico:
- Step 6 (complemento proteico) fell back to complementos[0] (frango)
  when no preferred magra was found
- Fix: if user has >= 2 explicit prefs, ONLY use preferred proteins
  as complement (repeat preferred rather than use non-preferred)

Problem 2 — Caloric deficit > 100 kcal:
- Added post-assembly caloric adjustment in TACO Builder
- If |generated - target| > 100 kcal, scale all items proportionally
  using TACO recalculation (not raw multiplication)

Also:
- LLM prompt now injects allowed protein list when preferences exist
  "PROTEÍNAS PERMITIDAS: {list}. Qualquer outra é PROIBIDA."
- Remove phantom carne_suina_magra duplicates from preferencias-map

Validated: 7-day x 2 scenarios (2000kcal, 2831kcal)
- Frango: 0/14 days (PASS)
- Lombo suíno: 14/14 days (PASS)
- Caloric diff: -26 kcal avg @2000, -44 kcal avg @2831 (PASS)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: improve spatial analyzer — bilateral filter, better grid detection

Replace Gaussian blur with bilateral filter to preserve color transitions,
lower Canny thresholds (15,60) to catch subtle edges in dark images, and
use cell-height-based row clustering instead of fixed 5% threshold.

Fixes merged regions (459px-wide blob from two items) and incorrect grid
detection (7x6 → correct 7x3). Adds IoU-based overlap merging and
filters canvas-spanning regions from grid inference.

Gem-boxes test: 20/21 items detected, 7x3 grid, no merges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: melhora tratamento de erros de rede vs autenticação

Problema anterior:
- Todos os erros "Failed to fetch" eram interpretados como token inválido
- Isso causava mensagens incorretas quando o erro era de rede ou backend

Correções:
- Captura erros de rede (Failed to fetch) no claude.ts com mensagem específica
- Remove conversão genérica de "Failed to fetch" → "Token inválido" no App.tsx
- Mantém mensagens específicas de 401 (token inválido) e 403 (sem acesso)
- Agora erros de rede mostram: "Erro de conexão. Verifique sua internet..."
- Erros de autenticação continuam mostrando mensagens específicas

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix(ISSUE-2168): get_next_id/check_id_collision now include archive files

Both methods previously scanned only the primary governance file, missing
IDs that had been moved to archive/governance/. This caused stale ID returns
when archival work was present (committed or uncommitted).

Fix: add ARCHIVE_PATHS class var + _get_archive_path() helper. Both
get_next_id() and check_id_collision() now also read the corresponding
archive file (non-blocking — archive read failures fall back gracefully).

commit.message:
Add best-practice guardrails

Enforce software best practices at three layers:

Prompt-level: planner, code-gen prompt, and
workspace CLAUDE.md now instruct git init -b main,
.gitignore, pinned deps, HTTPS, no hardcoded
secrets, no eval/exec/shell=True, specific
exceptions, context managers, and UTF-8 encoding.

Post-execution: regex scanner checks generated .py
files for violations (hardcoded API keys trigger
rewrite; bare except, eval, shell=True, plain HTTP,
and git init without -b are logged as warnings).

Project-level: multi-file projects are checked for
git main branch, .gitignore, README, and dependency
file. Warnings appear in VALIDATION.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(media): extractHlsUrl must return master playlist, not first variant

extractHlsUrl() returned the first .m3u8 found (e.g. _480p.m3u8),
which is a single-bitrate variant. The master playlist enables
adaptive bitrate switching across all 3 renditions (480p/720p/1080p).

Fix: collect all .m3u8 paths, prefer the one without variant suffix.
Falls back to first variant if no master is found.

fix: load saved conn_profile on startup and add CandidateG write-trigger for D2

BUG 1 — conn_profile.json not loading:
- StorageService.LoadFromFile now recovers from interrupted atomic saves by
  falling back to the .tmp file when the primary path is missing
- TryRestoreCachedProfile now trusts saved Pixart profiles when the HID device
  is still present on the system, rather than requiring an immediate successful
  battery read (which fails for passive-read methods like CandidateE on cold
  start before the device has been triggered)
- Added IHidDeviceService.IsDevicePresent() to verify device path existence
  without attempting a battery read

BUG 2 — CandidateG write-trigger for Model D2 Wireless:
- New probe candidate that sends explicit SetFeature/Write commands on col01
  with known Glorious battery request payloads, then reads the response from
  col05's input stream
- Tries three payload variants (0x83 Glorious command, 0x83 alt, 0x12 alt)
  via both SetFeature and Write, plus RID 0x03 as SetFeature fallback
- Added to probe chain after CandidateF, and to ReadBatteryPixart dispatch
- Stores SiblingDevicePath (col05) in profile for ongoing reads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: xbox-switch-bridge

 (#70)

* feat: add Xbox-to-Switch controller bridge for ESP32-S3

Implements a BLE-to-USB protocol bridge that lets an Xbox Series
wireless controller work as a wired Nintendo Switch Pro Controller.

Architecture:
- bluepad32_host: BLE gamepad host using Bluepad32 library
- button_mapper: Xbox→Switch input translation (A/B X/Y swap)
- switch_pro_usb: Pro Controller USB HID emulation via TinyUSB
  (VID 0x057E, PID 0x2009, 0x80 handshake protocol)

Requires ESP32-S3 for native USB OTG + Bluetooth LE.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ce4hajsQeVSNgrGkAwaMzS

* fix(xbox-switch-bridge): correct Switch Pro Controller protocol implementation

- Replace HID report descriptor with the real Pro Controller descriptor
  (captured from actual hardware, uses report ID 0x81 for USB replies)
- Fix USB handshake responses to use report ID 0x81 instead of 0x80
- Add proper SPI flash calibration data responses for stick centers
  (prevents stick drift from zero-filled calibration data)
- Fix Bluepad32 v4.x initialization: add btstack_init() and
  btstack_run_loop_execute() calls per upstream API requirements
- Set connection info byte to 0x8E (USB, Pro Controller, full report)
- Handle SPI flash reads for factory/user calibration and body colors

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ce4hajsQeVSNgrGkAwaMzS

* docs(xbox-switch-bridge): target Waveshare ESP32-S3-Zero as recommended board

Enable PSRAM in sdkconfig.defaults, add ESP32-S3-Zero flashing instructions
to WIRING.md, and update README with board-specific port and cable info.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Ce4hajsQeVSNgrGkAwaMzS

Add cost optimization, Apple/Google OAuth, and iOS support

commit.message:
fix Bearer null token causing jwt malformed error

The Authorization header was set once at startup with localStorage.getItem('token'),
which evaluates to "Bearer null" when no token exists. Replace with an axios
interceptor that reads the token dynamically and only sets the header when a
token is actually present.

  panels, buttons, timeline, skills, hobbies, and contact sections

  the full viewport instead of just the top two-thirds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Allow student/observer deletion when only registered for past events

Previously the user-facing DELETE /observers/{id} had no event-guard
at all, so observers could never be blocked — but also couldn't be
safely deleted because EventRegistrationObserver rows were left orphaned.

Changes:
- DELETE /observers/{id}: added the same upcoming-finalized guard as
  students — blocks deletion only when observer is in a finalized
  registration for an event whose date >= now. Also cleans up
  EventRegistrationObserver rows for past/non-finalized events first.
- DELETE /admin/users/{user_id}/observers/{id}: same guard + FK cleanup
  (admin panel Remove button now respects the same rule).
- DELETE /admin/users/{user_id}/students/{id}: added the same
  upcoming-event guard (was missing) + FK cleanup before deletion.

Result: students and observers whose only registrations are for past
events can be freely removed from the portal; upcoming finalized
registrations still block deletion with a clear error message.

commit.message:
Clarify .htaccess fix: CGIPassAuth On is the real fix for PHP-FPM

RewriteRule [E=HTTP_AUTHORIZATION:...] does not cross the FastCGI
boundary — CGIPassAuth On is what actually makes the Authorization
header available in $_SERVER on Hostinger PHP-FPM.
